disclaimer: This checklist is illustrative and ISACA-aligned but should be tailored to the specific scope, risk, and environment of the engagement. For educational use only.

cloud security.objective: Verify cloud adoption aligns with governance, risk appetite, and documented strategy.
Confirm the CSP/customer responsibility split is documented and understood.
Verify data is classified and encrypted at rest and in transit.
Assess IAM roles, least privilege, and federation for cloud consoles/APIs.
Review security groups, segmentation, and exposure of public endpoints.
Confirm cloud audit logs, alerting, and incident response coverage.
Review CSP certifications (SOC 2, ISO 27001) and contractual security obligations.
Assess backup, disaster recovery, and continuity for cloud workloads.
Evaluate baseline configs and cloud security posture management (CSPM).
Verify compliance with data location and regulatory requirements.

access management.objective: Confirm an approved access control policy exists and is current.
Test joiner/mover/leaver processes for timeliness and approval.
Assess role design and that access maps to job function.
Review controls over admin/privileged accounts (vaulting, session monitoring).
Verify periodic access reviews are performed and exceptions remediated.
Assess MFA coverage and password/credential policy enforcement.
Identify and evaluate SoD conflicts and compensating controls.
Confirm authentication and access events are logged and reviewed.
Test for stale, shared, or unowned accounts.
Review vendor/contractor access scope, approval, and removal.

data privacy.objective: Confirm policies map to applicable laws (e.g. GDPR, local privacy acts).
Verify personal data is inventoried and data flows documented.
Assess capture, storage, and withdrawal of consent.
Test processes for access, correction, and erasure requests.
Verify retention schedules and disposal of personal data.
Assess encryption, masking, and access restrictions on personal data.
Review third-party data processing agreements and safeguards.
Evaluate breach detection and regulatory notification procedures.
Confirm privacy is considered in system/change design.
Verify DPIAs are performed for high-risk processing.

network security.objective: Confirm an approved network security architecture and standards.
Review firewall rules, change control, and periodic rule reviews.
Assess segmentation between trust zones and critical assets.
Assess secure remote access, MFA, and split-tunneling controls.
Review wireless authentication, encryption, and rogue AP detection.
Evaluate scanning cadence and remediation SLAs.
Confirm network telemetry is collected, retained, and reviewed.
Assess protections for availability of network services.
Review baseline hardening of routers, switches, and network appliances.

general.objective: Confirm approved policies and standards exist for the domain.
Verify ownership and accountability are defined.
Assess whether domain risks are identified and rated.
Evaluate key controls for design and operating effectiveness.
Confirm relevant activity is logged, alerted, and reviewed.
Identify SoD conflicts and compensating controls.
Assess alignment with applicable laws, regulations, and frameworks (e.g. COBIT).
Review detection, response, and escalation processes.
Verify changes are authorized, tested, and documented.
Confirm records are retained to support audit evidence.
